The classification header has already been emitted. Do NOT emit it again. This skill handles **execution only** for STANDARD and STRICT tier tasks.\n>\n> If for any reason no classification header was emitted before this skill was invoked, emit one now using ONLY the tier values STRICT, STANDARD, LIGHT, or EXEMPT (no other values are valid).\n\n# /sc:task-unified - Unified Task Execution with Compliance\n\n## Purpose\n\nUnified command that merges orchestration capabilities with MCP compliance enforcement. Automatically classifies tasks into compliance tiers and enforces appropriate verification.\n\n**Key Benefits**:\n- Single command replaces confusion between `sc:task` and `sc:task-mcp`\n- Automatic tier classification with confidence scoring\n- Appropriate verification for each task type\n- Prevents over-engineering for trivial changes\n- Ensures safety for critical changes\n\n## Triggers\n\nUse `/sc:task` when:\n- Task involves code modifications with downstream impacts\n- Complexity score >0.6 with code modifications\n- Multi-file scope (>2 estimated affected files)\n- Security domain paths detected (auth/, security/, crypto/)\n- Refactoring or system-wide changes requested\n\n**Auto-Suggest Keywords**:\n- High confidence: \"implement feature\", \"refactor system\", \"fix security\", \"add authentication\", \"update database schema\"\n- Moderate confidence: \"add new\", \"create component\", \"update service\", \"modify API\"\n\n## Usage\n\n```bash\n/sc:task [description]                           # Auto-detect all dimensions\n/sc:task [description] --compliance strict       # Force STRICT tier\n/sc:task [description] --compliance light        # Force LIGHT tier\n/sc:task [description] --skip-compliance         # Bypass compliance (escape hatch)\n/sc:task [description] --verify auto             # Auto-select verification\n```\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\n### 0. Classification (Already Performed)\n\nClassification was handled by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The tier has been determined and the classification header has been emitted. Proceed directly to execution based on the classified tier.\n\n**Reference** — the tier keyword tables (for context only, do not re-classify):\n- **STRICT**: security, authentication, database, migration, refactor, breaking change, encrypt, token, session, oauth\n- **EXEMPT**: explain, search, commit, push, plan, discuss, brainstorm\n- **LIGHT**: typo, comment, whitespace, lint, docstring, formatting, minor\n- **STANDARD**: implement, add, create, update, fix, build, modify, change (default)\n\n### 2. Confidence Display (Human-Readable)\n\nAfter the mandatory classification header, show a human-readable summary:\n\n```\n**Tier: STANDARD** [████████░░] 80%\n\nClassified as STANDARD:\n- Keywords matched: add, implement\n- Confidence score: 0.78\n- Considered alternatives: STRICT (0.35)\n```\n\nIf confidence <70%, add prompt: \"⚠️ Low confidence. Override with: `--compliance [strict|standard|light|exempt]`\"\n\n**Note**: The machine-readable header (Section 0) is for telemetry/A/B testing. This human-readable display is for user understanding.\n\n### 3. Execution Phase\n\nExecute task according to tier requirements:\n\n**STRICT Execution**:\n1. Activate project (mcp__serena__activate_project)\n2. Verify git working directory clean (git status)\n3. Load codebase context (codebase-retrieval)\n4. Check relevant memories (list_memories -> read_memory)\n5. Identify all affected files and test files\n6. Make changes with full checklist\n7. Identify all files that import changed code\n8. Update all affected files\n9. Spawn verification agent (quality-engineer)\n10. Run comprehensive tests: `pytest [path] -v`\n11. Answer adversarial questions\n\n**STANDARD Execution**:\n1. Load context via codebase-retrieval\n2. Search downstream impacts (find_referencing_symbols OR grep)\n3. Make changes\n4. Run affected tests OR document manual verification\n5. Verify basic functionality\n\n**LIGHT Execution**:\n1. Quick scope check (files/lines within bounds)\n2. Make changes\n3. Quick sanity check (syntax valid, no obvious errors)\n4. Proceed with judgment\n\n**EXEMPT Execution**:\n1. Execute immediately\n2. No verification overhead\n\n### 4. Verification Phase\n\nRoute to appropriate verification based on tier and paths:\n\n| Compliance Tier | Verification Method | Token Cost | Timeout |\n|-----------------|---------------------|------------|---------|\n| STRICT | Sub-agent (quality-engineer) | 3-5K | 60s |\n| STANDARD | Direct test execution | 300-500 | 30s |\n| LIGHT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n| EXEMPT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n\n**Critical Path Override**: Paths matching `auth/`, `security/`, `crypto/`, `models/`, `migrations/` always trigger CRITICAL verification regardless of compliance tier.\n\n**Trivial Path Override**: Paths matching `*.md`, `docs/`, `*test*.py` may skip verification.\n\n### 5. Feedback Collection\n\nAfter completion, collect implicit feedback:\n- Track if user overrode tier (implicit classification feedback)\n- Note smooth completion vs errors (quality signal)\n- Store for calibration learning\n\n## MCP Integration\n\n**Required Servers by Tier**:\n- STRICT: Sequential, Serena (fallback not allowed)\n- STANDARD: Sequential, Context7 (fallback allowed)\n- LIGHT: None required (fallback allowed)\n- EXEMPT: None required\n\n**Circuit Breaker Behavior**:\n- If required servers unavailable for STRICT tier, block task execution\n- For other tiers, use fallbacks with noted limitations\n\n## Tool Coordination\n\n**Planning Phase**:\n1. TodoWrite: Create task breakdown\n2. codebase-retrieval: Load context\n3. list_memories / read_memory: Check project state\n\n**Execution Phase**:\n1. Edit/MultiEdit/Write: Make changes\n2. Grep/Glob: Find references\n3. find_referencing_symbols: Trace dependencies\n\n**Verification Phase**:\n1. Task (quality-engineer): Spawn verification agent (STRICT only)\n2. Bash: Run tests directly (STANDARD)\n3. think_about_task_adherence: Reflect on completeness\n\n**Completion Phase**:\n1. write_memory: Save session state\n2. think_about_whether_you_are_done: Final check\n\n## Examples\n\n### STRICT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"implement user authentication with JWT\"\n\n-> Classified as STRICT (security domain, authentication keyword)\n-> Full 6-category checklist enforced\n-> Verification agent spawned\n-> Adversarial questions answered\n```\n\n### STANDARD Task\n```\n/sc:task \"add pagination to user list\"\n\n-> Classified as STANDARD (add keyword, typical feature)\n-> Context loaded before editing\n-> Downstream impacts checked\n-> Direct test execution\n```\n\n### LIGHT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"fix typo in README\"\n\n-> Classified as LIGHT (trivial keyword, documentation path)\n-> Quick sanity check only\n-> No verification delay\n```\n\n### EXEMPT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"explain how the auth flow works\"\n\n-> Classified as EXEMPT (explain pattern detected)\n-> Immediate execution\n-> No compliance overhead\n```\n\n### Override Example\n```\n/sc:task \"update config file\" --compliance strict\n\n-> User override to STRICT regardless of classification\n-> Full verification enforced\n```\n\n## Boundaries\n\n**Will:**\n- Classify tasks into appropriate compliance tiers\n- Enforce tier-appropriate verification requirements\n- Provide confidence scoring with rationale\n- Track feedback for continuous calibration\n- Support user overrides with justification\n\n**Will Not:**\n- Skip safety-critical verification for STRICT tasks\n- Apply STRICT overhead to genuinely trivial changes\n- Override user's explicit compliance choice\n- Proceed with <70% confidence without user confirmation\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n| Metric | Target | Measurement |\n|--------|--------|-------------|\n| Tier classification accuracy | >=80% | User feedback on appropriateness |\n| User confusion rate | <10% | \"Which command?\" questions eliminated |\n| Skip rate (--skip-compliance) | <12% | Override tracking |\n| Regression prevention | >=85% | Post-verification bug detection |\n| STRICT tier overhead | <25% | Execution telemetry |\n\n## Configuration References\n\n- Keywords: `config/tier-keywords.yaml`\n- Verification routing: `config/verification-routing.yaml`\n- Acceptance criteria: `config/tier-acceptance-criteria.yaml`\n- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   110→# Semantic check helper functions (FR-047, AC-004)\n   111→# All return tuple[bool, str] — (passed, diagnostic_message)\n   112→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   113→\n   114→\n   115→def has_valid_yaml_config(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n   116→    \"\"\"Check that YAML frontmatter contains workflow_path, cli_name, output_dir.\"\"\"\n   117→    required = {\"workflow_path\", \"cli_name\", \"output_dir\"}\n   118→    found: set[str] = set()\n   119→    in_frontmatter = False\n   120→    lines = content.splitlines()\n   121→    fence_count = 0\n   122→    for line in lines:\n   123→        stripped = line.strip()\n   124→        if stripped == \"---\":\n   125→            fence_count += 1\n   126→            if fence_count == 1:\n   127→                in_frontmatter = True\n   128→            elif fence_count == 2:\n   129→                break\n   130→            continue\n   131→        if in_frontmatter and \":\" in stripped:\n   132→            key = stripped.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n   133→            if key:\n   134→                found.add(key)\n   135→    missing = required - found\n   136→    if missing:\n   137→        return False, f\"Missing required config fields: {sorted(missing)}\"\n   138→    return True, \"\"\n   139→\n   140→\n   141→def has_component_inventory(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n   142→    \"\"\"Check that inventory lists ≥1 component with SKILL.md reference.\"\"\"\n    \"\"\"Check that approval_status field is present (approved/rejected/pending).\"\"\"\n   176→    if re.search(r\"approval_status\\s*:\", content):\n   177→        return True, \"\"\n   178→ 188→def has_zero_placeholders(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n   189→    \"\"\"Check that no {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels remain.\"\"\"\n   190→    match = re.search(r\"\\{\\{SC_PLACEHOLDER:[^}]*\\}\\}\", content)\n   191→    if match:\n   192→        return False, f\"Placeholder sentinel found: {match.group(0)}\"\n   193→    return True, \"\"\n   194→\n   195→\n   196→def has_brainstorm_section(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n   197→    \"\"\"Check that Section 12 (brainstorm / gaps) is present.\"\"\"\n   198→    # Accept various heading patterns for brainstorm/gaps section\n   199→    patterns = [\n   200→        r\"(?:^|\\n)#{1,3}\\s+(?:Section\\s+12|Brainstorm|Gaps|Gap Analysis|Missing)\",\n   201→    ]\n   202→    for pattern in 207→\n   208→def has_quality_scores(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n   209→    \"\"\"Check for quality score fields: clarity, completeness, testability, consistency, overall.\"\"\"\n   210→    required_scores = [\"clarity\", \"completeness\", \"testability\", \"consistency\", \"overall\"]\n   211→    missing = []\n   212→    for score in required_scores:\n   213→        if not {missing}\"\n   217→    return True, \"\"\n   218→\n   219→\n   220→def has_criticals_addressed(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n   221→    \"\"\"Check that all CRITICAL items are marked [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED].\"\"\"\n   222→    # Find all CRITICAL markers\n   223→    critical_markers = re.findall(r\"CRITICAL\", content)\n   224→    if not critical_markers:\n   225→ r\"CRITICAL(?!.*?\\[(?:INCORPORATED|DISMISSED)\\])\",\n   229→        content,\n   230→    )\n   231→    if unresolved:\n   232→        return False, f\"Found {len(unresolved)} CRITICAL item(s) not marked [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\"\n   233→    return True, \"\"\n   234→\n   235→\n   236→def has_return_type_pattern(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n   237→    \"\"\"Check for return type pattern (tuple[bool, str] or return type annotation).\"\"\"\n   238→    if re.search(r\"tuple\\[bool,\\s*str\\]|-> tuple|return.*bool.*str\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n   239→        return True, \"\"\n   240→    return False, \"Missing return type pattern (tuple[bool, str])\"\n   241→\n   242→\n   243→def has_step_count_consistency(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n   244→    \"\"\"Check that step_mapping count matches declared steps.\"\"\"\n   245→    # Look for a declared step count and a step_mapping/step_definitions section\n   246→    count_match = re.search(r\"(?:pipeline_steps|step_count|steps)\\s*:\\s*(\\d+)\", content, re.IGNORECASE)\n   247→    if not count_match:\n   248→        return True, \"\"  # Can't verify without declared count, pass\n   249→    declared = int(count_match.group(1))\n   250→    # Count actual step headings\n   251→    actual_steps = len(re.findall(r\"###\\s+Step\\s+\\d+:\", content, re.IGNORECASE))\n   252→    if actual_steps == 0:\n   253→        return True, \"\"  # No step headings to count against\n   254→    if actual_steps != declared:\n   255→        return False, f\"Step count mismatch: declared {declared} but found {actual_steps} step definitions\"\n   256→    return True, \"\"\n   257→\n   258→\n   259→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   260→# identifier (e.g. 'G-003').\n        check_name: Name of the failing semantic check.\n        diagnostic: Diagnostic message from the check.\n        artifact_path: Path to the artifact that failed the {remediation}\"\n    )\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Semantic check helper functions (FR-047, AC-004)\n# All return tuple[bool, str] — (passed, frontmatter contains workflow_path, cli_name, output_dir.\"\"\"\n    required = {\"workflow_path\", \"cli_name\", \"output_dir\"}\n    found: set[str] = set()\n    in_frontmatter = False\n    lines = content.splitlines()\n    fence_count = 0\n    for line in lines:\n        stripped = line.strip()\n        if stripped == \"---\":\n            fence_count += 1\n            if fence_count == 1:\n                in_frontmatter = True\n            elif fence_count == 2:\n                break\n            continue\n        if in_frontmatter and \":\" in stripped:\n            key = \"\"\n\n\ndef has_required_analysis_sections(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for all 7 required section headers in analysis output.\"\"\"\n    required_sections = [\n        \"Source Components\",\n        \"Step Graph\",\n        \"Parallel Groups\",\n        \"Gates Summary\",\n        \"Data Flow\",\n        \"Classifications\",\n        \"Recommendations\",\n    ]\n  return False, \"Missing EXIT_RECOMMENDATION marker\"\n\n\ndef has_zero_placeholders(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that no {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels remain.\"\"\"\n    match = re.search(r\"\\{\\{SC_PLACEHOLDER:[^}]*\\}\\}\", content)\n    if match:\n        return False, f\"Placeholder sentinel found: {match.group(0)}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef patterns = [\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)#{1,3}\\s+(?:Section\\s+12|Brainstorm|Gaps|Gap Analysis|Missing)\",\n    ]\n    for pattern in patterns:\n        if re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing brainstorm/gaps section (Section 12)\"\n\n\ndef has_quality_scores(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for quality score fields: # Check that each CRITICAL has an adjacent [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\n    unresolved = re.findall(\n        r\"CRITICAL(?!.*?\\[(?:INCORPORATED|DISMISSED)\\])\",\n        content,\n    )\n    if re.search(r\"(?:pipeline_steps|step_count|steps)\\s*:\\s*(\\d+)\", content, re.IGNORECASE)\n    if not count_match:\n        return True, \"\"  # Can't verify without declared count, pass\n    declared = int(count_match.group(1))\n    # Count actual step headings\n    actual_steps = len(re.findall(r\"###\\s+Step\\s+\\d+:\", content, re.IGNORECASE))\n    if actual_steps == 0:\n        return True, \"\"  # No step headings to count against\n    if actual_steps != declared:\n        return False, f\"Step count mismatch: declared {declared} but found {actual_steps} step definitions\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Gate criteria constants (G-000 through G-011 as per spec)\n# \"\"\"Return True if convergence_state is a terminal state (converged or blocked).\"\"\"\n    match = re.search(r\"convergence_state\\s*:\\s*(\\w+)\", content)\n    if not match:\n        return ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef gate_analyze_workflow(artifact_path: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Gate for analyze-workflow step (STRICT).\n\n    Checks:\n    - File exists\n    - Minimum 5 section headers\n    \"\"\"\n    if not artifact_path.exists():\n        return False, f\"Artifact not found: {artifact_path}\"\n fence_count = 0\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        if line.strip() == \"---\":\n            fence_count += 1\n            if fence_count == 1:\n                in_fm = True\n            elif fence_count == 2:\n                break\n            continue\n        if in_fm and \":\" in line:\n            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n            if key:\n dataclass, field\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PromptContext\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PromptContext:\n    \"\"\"Context passed to all prompt builders.\"\"\"\n\n    workflow_path: Path\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass BasePromptBuilder:\n    \"\"\"Base class for all CLI Portify prompt builders.\n\n    Subclasses must implement:\n    - step_name: str\n    - _body() -> str  (main prompt body)\n    - required_frontmatter() -> list[str]\n    - output_contract() -> list[str]\n    - input_artifacts() -> list[Path]\n    \"\"\"\n\n    step_name: str = \"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, ctx: PromptContext) -> None:\n        self.ctx = ctx\n\n    def input_artifacts(self) -> list[Path]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of prior-step artifact Paths referenced by this prompt.\"\"\"\n        return []\n\n    def required_frontmatter(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of required YAML frontmatter field names.\"\"\"\n        return []\n\n    def output_contract(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of output contract requirements.\"\"\"\n        return []\n\n    def _body(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the main prompt body text.\"\"\"\n        return \"\"\n\n    def build(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full prompt string with @path references, output contract, and frontmatter.\"\"\"\n        parts: list[str] = []\n\n        # @path references for prior artifacts\n        for artifact in self.input_artifacts():\n            parts.append(f\"@{artifact.resolve()}\")\n\n        # Main body\n        body = self._body()\n        if body:\n            parts.append(body)\n\n        # Required Frontmatter section\n        fm_fields = self.required_frontmatter()\n        if fm_fields:\n            fm_lines = [\"\", \"## Required Frontmatter\", \"```yaml\", \"---\"]\n            for f in fm_fields:\n                fm_lines.append(f\"{f}: <value>\")\n            fm_lines += [\"---\", \"```\"]\n            parts.append(\"\\n\".join(fm_lines))\n\n        # Output Contract section\n        oc_items = self.output_contract()\n        if oc_items:\n            oc_lines = [\"\", \"## Output Contract\"]\n            for item in oc_items:\n                oc_lines.append(f\"- {item}\")\n            parts.append(\"\\n\".join(oc_lines))\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(parts)\n\n    def build_retry(\n        self,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        remaining_placeholders: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build a retry prompt that includes the original prompt plus retry context.\"\"\"\n        base = self.build()\n        retry_parts = [base, \"\", \"## RETRY CONTEXT\", f\"Previous attempt failed: {failure_reason}\"]\n\n        if remaining_placeholders:\n            retry_parts.append(\"\")\n            retry_parts.append(\"The following placeholder sentinels MUST be resolved:\")\n            for ph in remaining_placeholders:\n                retry_parts.append(f\"  - {ph}\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(retry_parts)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# AnalyzeWorkflowPrompt\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass AnalyzeWorkflowPrompt(BasePromptBuilder):\n    \"\"\"Prompt builder for the analyze-workflow step.\"\"\"\n\n    step_name = \"analyze-workflow\"\n\n    def input_artifacts(self) -> list[Path]:\n \"component_count\", \"analysis_sections\"]\n\n    def output_contract(self) -> list[str]:\n        return [\n            \"Include EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE or EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT at end of output\",\n            \"Include all 5 required section headers (##)\",\n            \"Include component inventory reference\",\n        ]\n\n    def _body(self) -> str:\n        return (\n            f\"Analyze the workflow for {self.ctx.cli_name} (source skill: {self.ctx.source_skill}).\\n\"\n            f\"Review the component inventory and produce a comprehensive workflow analysis.\\n\"\n            f\"Include sections: Source Components, Step Graph, Parallel Groups, Gates Summary, Data Flow, \"\n            f\"Classifications, Complexity Assessment.\\n\"\n        )\n\n\n# return [\n            self.ctx.work_dir / \"results\" / \"portify-analysis.md\",\n            self.ctx.work_dir / \"results\" / \"component-inventory.md\",\n        ]\n\n    def builder is registered for step_name.\n    \"\"\"\n    if step_name not in PROMPT_BUILDERS:\n        raise KeyError(f\"No prompt builder registered for step: '{step_name}'\")\n    return ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass BasePromptBuilder:\n    \"\"\"Base class for all CLI Portify prompt builders.\n\n    Subclasses must implement:\n    - step_name: str\n    - _body() -> str  (main prompt body)\n    - required_frontmatter() -> list[str]\n    - output_contract() -> list[str]\n    - input_artifacts() -> list[Path]\n    \"\"\"\n\n Paths referenced by this prompt.\"\"\"\n        return []\n\n    def required_frontmatter(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of required YAML frontmatter field names.\"\"\"\n        return []\n\n    def output_contract(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of output contract requirements.\"\"\"\n        return []\n\n    def _body(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return the main prompt body text.\"\"\"\n        return \"\"\n\n    def build(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full prompt string with @path references, output contract, and frontmatter.\"\"\"\n        parts: list[str] = []\n\n        # @path references for prior artifacts\n        for artifact in self.input_artifacts():\n            parts.append(f\"@{artifact.resolve()}\")\n\n        # Main body\n        body = self._body()\n        if body:\n            parts.append(body)\n\n        # Required Frontmatter section\n        fm_fields = self.required_frontmatter()\n        if fm_fields:\n            fm_lines = [\"\", \"## Required Frontmatter\", \"```yaml\", \"---\"]\n            for f in fm_fields:\n                fm_lines.append(f\"{f}: <value>\")\n            fm_lines += [\"---\", \"```\"]\n            parts.append(\"\\n\".join(fm_lines))\n\n        # Output Contract section\n        oc_items = self.output_contract()\n        if oc_items:\n            oc_lines = [\"\", \"## Output Contract\"]\n            for item in oc_items:\n                oc_lines.append(f\"- {item}\")\n            parts.append(\"\\n\".join(oc_lines))\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(parts)\n\n    def build_retry(\n        self,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        remaining_placeholders: Optional[list[str]] = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build a retry prompt that includes the original prompt plus retry context.\"\"\"\n        base = self.build()\n        retry_parts = [base, \"\", \"## RETRY CONTEXT\", f\"Previous attempt failed: {failure_reason}\"]\n\n        if remaining_placeholders:\n            retry_parts.append(\"\")\n            retry_parts.append(\"The following placeholder sentinels MUST be resolved:\")\n            for ph in remaining_placeholders:\n                retry_parts.append(f\"  - {ph}\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(retry_parts)\n\n\n# SemanticCheck\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# GateFailure dataclass (T04.03, AC-004)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass GateFailure:\n    \"\"\"Structured representation of a gate failure.\n\n    Attributes:\n        gate_id: Gate identifier (e.g. 'G-003').\n        check_name: Name of the failing semantic check.\n        diagnostic: Human-readable diagnostic message.\n        artifact_path: Path to the artifact that failed the gate.\n        tier: Enforcement tier ('STRICT', 'STANDARD', 'LIGHT', 'EXEMPT').\n    \"\"\"\n\n    gate_id: str\n    check_name: str\n    diagnostic: str\n    artifact_path: str\n    tier: Literal[\"STRICT\", \"STANDARD\", \"LIGHT\", \"EXEMPT\"] = \"STANDARD\"\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Gate diagnostics formatting (T04.03)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# Remediation hints by check name\n_REMEDIATION_HINTS: dict[str, str] = {\n    \"has_valid_yaml_config\": \"Add required fields (workflow_path, cli_name, output_dir) to the YAML config\",\n    \"has_component_inventory\": \"Ensure the workflow directory contains at least one component with SKILL.md\",\n    \"has_required_analysis_sections\": \"Add all 7 required sections: Source Components, Step Graph, Parallel Groups, Gates Summary, Data Flow, Classifications, Recommendations\",\n    \"has_approval_status\": \"Add 'approval_status: pending|approved|rejected' to the artifact frontmatter\",\n    \"has_exit_recommendation\": \"Add 'EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE' or 'EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT' to the artifact\",\n    \"has_zero_placeholders\": \"Replace all {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels with actual content\",\n    \"has_brainstorm_section\": \"Add Section 12 (Brainstorm / Gaps Analysis) to the artifact\",\n    \"has_quality_scores\": \"Add quality score fields (clarity, completeness, testability, consistency, overall) to the artifact\",\n    \"has_criticals_addressed\": \"Resolve all CRITICAL items by marking them [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\",\n    \"has_return_type_pattern\": \"Ensure return type pattern tuple[bool, str] is present in the artifact\",\n    \"has_step_count_consistency\": \"Reconcile declared step count with actual step definitions in the artifact\",\n    \"section_count\": \"Add more section headers (##) to reach the minimum required count\",\n    \"zero_placeholders\": \"Replace all {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels with actual content\",\n    \"convergence_terminal\": \"Set convergence_state to 'converged' or 'blocked' before passing this gate\",\n}\n\n_DEFAULT_REMEDIATION = \"Review the artifact against the gate specification and correct the identified issue\"\n\n\ndef format_gate_failure(\n    gate_id: str,\n    check_name: str,\n    diagnostic: str,\n    artifact_path: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format a gate failure into a human-readable multi-line string.\n\n    Args:\n        gate_id: Gate identifier (e.g. 'G-003').\n        check_name: Name of the failing semantic check.\n        diagnostic: Diagnostic message from the check.\n        artifact_path: Path to the artifact that failed the gate.\n\n    Returns:\n        Multi-line string with gate_id, check_name, artifact_path, and remediation hint.\n    \"\"\"\n    remediation = _REMEDIATION_HINTS.get(check_name, {remediation}\"\n    )\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Semantic check helper functions (FR-047, AC-004)\n# All return tuple[bool, str] — (passed, diagnostic_message)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef has_valid_yaml_config(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that YAML frontmatter contains workflow_path, cli_name, output_dir.\"\"\"\n    required = {\"workflow_path\", \"cli_name\", \"output_dir\"}\n    found: set[str] = set()\n    in_frontmatter = False\n    lines = content.splitlines()\n    fence_count = 0\n    for line in lines:\n        stripped = line.strip()\n        if stripped == \"---\":\n            fence_count += 1\n            if fence_count == 1:\n                in_frontmatter = True\n            elif fence_count == 2:\n                break\n            continue\n        if in_frontmatter and \":\" in stripped:\n            key = stripped.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n            if key:\n                found.add(key)\n    missing = required - found\n    if missing:\n        return False, f\"Missing required config fields: {sorted(missing)}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_component_inventory(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that inventory lists ≥1 component with SKILL.md reference.\"\"\"\n    # Check for at least one component entry\n    has_components = bool(\n        re.search(r\"component_count:\\s*[1-9]\", content)\n        or re.search(r\"##\\s+.*(Skill|Component|Inventory)\", content, re.IGNORECASE)\n        or re.search(r\"SKILL\\.md\", content)\n    )\n    if not has_components:\n        return False, \"Inventory does not list any components with SKILL.md\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_required_analysis_sections(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for all 7 required section headers in analysis output.\"\"\"\n    required_sections = [\n        \"Source Components\",\n        \"Step Graph\",\n        \"Parallel Groups\",\n        \"Gates Summary\",\n        \"Data Flow\",\n        \"Classifications\",\n        \"Recommendations\",\n    ]\n f\"Missing required analysis sections: {missing}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_approval_status(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that approval_status field is present (approved/rejected/pending).\"\"\"\n    if re.search(r\"approval_status\\s*:\", content):\n        return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing required field: approval_status\"\n\n\ndef has_exit_recommendation(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that EXIT_RECOMMENDATION marker is present.\"\"\"\n    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION\" in content:\n        return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing EXIT_RECOMMENDATION marker\"\n\n\ndef has_zero_placeholders(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that no {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels remain.\"\"\"\n    match = re.search(r\"\\{\\{SC_PLACEHOLDER:[^}]*\\}\\}\", content)\n    if match:\n        return False, f\"Placeholder sentinel found: {match.group(0)}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_brainstorm_section(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that Section 12 (brainstorm / gaps) is present.\"\"\"\n    # Accept various heading patterns for brainstorm/gaps section\n    patterns = [\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)#{1,3}\\s+(?:Section\\s+12|Brainstorm|Gaps|Gap Analysis|Missing)\",\n    ]\n    for pattern in patterns:\n        if re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing brainstorm/gaps section (Section 12)\"\n\n\ndef has_quality_scores(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for quality score fields: in required_scores:\n        if not re.search(rf\"\\b{score}\\b\\s*:\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            missing.append(score)\n    if missing:\n        return False, f\"Missing quality score fields: {missing}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_criticals_addressed(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that all CRITICAL items are marked [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED].\"\"\"\n    # Find all CRITICAL markers\n    critical_markers = re.findall(r\"CRITICAL\", content)\n    if not critical_markers:\n        return True, \"\"  # No criticals to address\n    # Check that each CRITICAL has an adjacent [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\n    unresolved = re.findall(\n        r\"CRITICAL(?!.*?\\[(?:INCORPORATED|DISMISSED)\\])\",\n        content,\n    )\n    if unresolved:\n        return False, f\"Found {len(unresolved)} CRITICAL item(s) not marked [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_return_type_pattern(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for return type pattern (tuple[bool, str] or return type annotation).\"\"\"\n    if re.search(r\"tuple\\[bool,\\s*str\\]|-> tuple|return.*bool.*str\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n        return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing return type pattern (tuple[bool, str])\"\n\n\ndef has_step_count_consistency(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check re.search(r\"(?:pipeline_steps|step_count|steps)\\s*:\\s*(\\d+)\", content, re.IGNORECASE)\n    if not count_match:\n        return True, \"\"  # Can't verify without declared count, pass\n    declared = int(count_match.group(1))\n    # Count actual step headings\n    actual_steps = len(re.findall(r\"###\\s+Step\\s+\\d+:\", content, re.IGNORECASE))\n    if actual_steps == 0:\n        return True, \"\"  # No step headings to count against\n    if actual_steps != declared:\n        return False, f\"Step count mismatch: declared {declared} but found {actual_steps} step definitions\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Gate criteria constants (G-000 through G-011 as per spec)\n# pipeline steps:\n  G-000 validate-config         EXEMPT  — config YAML valid\n  G-001 discover-components     STANDARD — inventory ≥1 component with SKILL.md\n  G-002 analyze-workflow-draft  STANDARD models-gates-design (G-006),\nprompts-executor-design (G-007), pipeline-spec-assembly (G-008).\n\nNFR-004: All gate functions return tuple[bool, str].\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Literal\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import GateCriteria, GateMode, SemanticCheck\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# GateFailure dataclass (T04.03, AC-004)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass GateFailure:\n    \"\"\"Structured representation of a gate failure.\n\n    Attributes:\n        gate_id: Gate identifier (e.g. 'G-003').\n        check_name: Name of the failing semantic check.\n        diagnostic: Human-readable diagnostic message.\n        artifact_path: Path to the artifact that failed the gate.\n        tier: Enforcement tier ('STRICT', 'STANDARD', 'LIGHT', 'EXEMPT').\n    \"\"\"\n\n    gate_id: str\n    check_name: str\n    diagnostic: str\n    artifact_path: str\n    tier: Literal[\"STRICT\", \"STANDARD\", \"LIGHT\", \"EXEMPT\"] = \"STANDARD\"\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Gate diagnostics formatting (T04.03)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# Remediation hints by check name\n_REMEDIATION_HINTS: dict[str, str] = {\n    \"has_valid_yaml_config\": \"Add required fields (workflow_path, cli_name, output_dir) to the YAML config\",\n    \"has_component_inventory\": \"Ensure the workflow directory contains at least one component with SKILL.md\",\n    \"has_required_analysis_sections\": \"Add all 7 required sections: Source Components, Step Graph, Parallel Groups, Gates Summary, Data Flow, Classifications, Recommendations\",\n    \"has_approval_status\": \"Add 'approval_status: pending|approved|rejected' to the artifact frontmatter\",\n    \"has_exit_recommendation\": \"Add 'EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE' or 'EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT' to the artifact\",\n    \"has_zero_placeholders\": \"Replace all {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels with actual content\",\n    \"has_brainstorm_section\": \"Add Section 12 (Brainstorm / Gaps Analysis) to the artifact\",\n    \"has_quality_scores\": \"Add quality score fields (clarity, completeness, testability, consistency, overall) to the artifact\",\n    \"has_criticals_addressed\": \"Resolve all CRITICAL items by marking them [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\",\n    \"has_return_type_pattern\": \"Ensure return type pattern tuple[bool, str] is present in the artifact\",\n    \"has_step_count_consistency\": \"Reconcile declared step count with actual step definitions in the artifact\",\n    \"section_count\": \"Add more section headers (##) to reach the minimum required count\",\n    \"zero_placeholders\": \"Replace all {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels with actual content\",\n    \"convergence_terminal\": \"Set convergence_state to 'converged' or 'blocked' before passing this gate\",\n}\n\n_DEFAULT_REMEDIATION = \"Review the artifact against the gate specification and correct the identified issue\"\n\n\ndef format_gate_failure(\n    gate_id: str,\n    check_name: str,\n    diagnostic: str,\n    artifact_path: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format a gate failure into a human-readable multi-line string.\n\n    Args:\n        gate_id: Gate identifier (e.g. 'G-003').\n        check_name: Name of the failing semantic check.\n        diagnostic: Diagnostic message from the check.\n        artifact_path: Path to the artifact that failed the gate.\n\n    Returns:\n        Multi-line string with gate_id, check_name, artifact_path, and remediation hint.\n    \"\"\"\n    remediation = _REMEDIATION_HINTS.get(check_name, _DEFAULT_REMEDIATION)\n    return (\n        f\"Gate {gate_id} FAILED: {check_name}\\n\"\n        f\"  Artifact: {artifact_path}\\n\"\n        f\"  Reason: {diagnostic}\\n\"\n        f\"  Fix: {remediation}\"\n    )\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Semantic check helper functions (FR-047, AC-004)\n# All return tuple[bool, str] — (passed, diagnostic_message)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef has_valid_yaml_config(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that YAML frontmatter contains workflow_path, cli_name, output_dir.\"\"\"\n    required = {\"workflow_path\", \"cli_name\", \"output_dir\"}\n    found: set[str] = set()\n    in_frontmatter = False\n    lines = content.splitlines()\n    fence_count = 0\n    for line in lines:\n        stripped = line.strip()\n        if stripped == \"---\":\n            fence_count += 1\n            if fence_count == 1:\n                in_frontmatter = True\n            elif fence_count == 2:\n                break\n            continue\n        if in_frontmatter and \":\" in stripped:\n            key = stripped.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n            if key:\n                found.add(key)\n    missing = required - found\n    if missing:\n        return False, f\"Missing required config fields: {sorted(missing)}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_component_inventory(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that inventory lists ≥1 component with SKILL.md reference.\"\"\"\n    # Check for at least one component entry\n    has_components = bool(\n        re.search(r\"component_count:\\s*[1-9]\", content)\n        or re.search(r\"##\\s+.*(Skill|Component|Inventory)\", content, re.IGNORECASE)\n        or re.search(r\"SKILL\\.md\", content)\n    )\n    if not has_components:\n        return False, \"Inventory does not list any components with SKILL.md\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_required_analysis_sections(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for all 7 required section headers in analysis output.\"\"\"\n    required_sections = [\n        \"Source Components\",\n        \"Step Graph\",\n        \"Parallel Groups\",\n        \"Gates Summary\",\n        \"Data Flow\",\n        \"Classifications\",\n        \"Recommendations\",\n    ]\n    missing = []\n    for section in required_sections:\n        if not re.search(re.escape(section), content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            missing.append(section)\n    if missing:\n        return False, f\"Missing required analysis sections: {missing}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_approval_status(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that approval_status field is present (approved/rejected/pending).\"\"\"\n    if re.search(r\"approval_status\\s*:\", content):\n        return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing required field: approval_status\"\n\n\ndef has_exit_recommendation(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that EXIT_RECOMMENDATION marker is present.\"\"\"\n    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION\" in content:\n        return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing EXIT_RECOMMENDATION marker\"\n\n\ndef has_zero_placeholders(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that no {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels remain.\"\"\"\n    match = re.search(r\"\\{\\{SC_PLACEHOLDER:[^}]*\\}\\}\", content)\n    if match:\n        return False, f\"Placeholder sentinel found: {match.group(0)}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_brainstorm_section(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that Section 12 (brainstorm / gaps) is present.\"\"\"\n    # Accept various heading patterns for brainstorm/gaps section\n    patterns = [\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)#{1,3}\\s+(?:Section\\s+12|Brainstorm|Gaps|Gap Analysis|Missing)\",\n    ]\n    for pattern in patterns:\n        if re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing brainstorm/gaps section (Section 12)\"\n\n\ndef has_quality_scores(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for quality score fields: clarity, completeness, testability, consistency, overall.\"\"\"\n    required_scores = [\"clarity\", \"completeness\", \"testability\", \"consistency\", \"overall\"]\n    missing = []\n    for score in required_scores:\n        if not re.search(rf\"\\b{score}\\b\\s*:\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            missing.append(score)\n    if missing:\n        return False, f\"Missing quality score fields: {missing}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_criticals_addressed(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that all CRITICAL items are marked [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED].\"\"\"\n    # Find all CRITICAL markers\n    critical_markers = re.findall(r\"CRITICAL\", content)\n    if not critical_markers:\n        return True, \"\"  # No criticals to address\n    # Check that each CRITICAL has an adjacent [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\n    unresolved = re.findall(\n        r\"CRITICAL(?!.*?\\[(?:INCORPORATED|DISMISSED)\\])\",\n        content,\n    )\n    if unresolved:\n        return False, f\"Found {len(unresolved)} CRITICAL item(s) not marked [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_return_type_pattern(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for return type pattern (tuple[bool, str] or return type annotation).\"\"\"\n    if re.search(r\"tuple\\[bool,\\s*str\\]|-> tuple|return.*bool.*str\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n        return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing return type pattern (tuple[bool, str])\"\n\n\ndef has_step_count_consistency(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that step_mapping count matches declared steps.\"\"\"\n    # Look for a declared step count and a step_mapping/step_definitions section\n    count_match = re.search(r\"(?:pipeline_steps|step_count|steps)\\s*:\\s*(\\d+)\", content, re.IGNORECASE)\n    if not count_match:\n        return True, \"\"  # Can't verify without declared count, pass\n    declared = int(count_match.group(1))\n    # Count actual step headings\n    actual_steps = len(re.findall(r\"###\\s+Step\\s+\\d+:\", content, re.IGNORECASE))\n    if actual_steps == 0:\n        return True, \"\"  # No step headings to count against\n    if actual_steps != declared:\n        return False, f\"Step count mismatch: declared {declared} but found {actual_steps} step definitions\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Gate criteria constants (G-000 through G-011 as per spec)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# G-000: validate-config (EXEMPT)\nVALIDATE_CONFIG_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[],\n    min_lines=0,\n    enforcement_tier=\"EXEMPT\",\n    semantic_checks=None,\n)\n\n# G-001: discover-components (STANDARD)\nDISCOVER_COMPONENTS_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"source_skill\", \"component_count\"],\n    min_lines=5,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n    semantic_checks=None,\n)\n\n# G-002: (not mapped to named step — EXIT_RECOMMENDATION present, STANDARD)\n# G-003: analyze-workflow (STRICT)\nANALYZE_WORKFLOW_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"step\", \"source_skill\", \"cli_name\", \"component_count\", \"analysis_sections\"],\n    min_lines=20,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"section_count\",\n            check_fn=lambda c: _check_min_sections(c, 5),\n            failure_message=\"Analysis must contain at least 5 section headers (##)\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n\n# G-004: (approval_status gate, STANDARD)\n# G-005: design-pipeline (STRICT)\nDESIGN_PIPELINE_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"step\", \"source_skill\", \"cli_name\", \"pipeline_steps\", \"gate_count\"],\n    min_lines=30,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=None,\n)\n\n# G-006: (return type pattern, STANDARD)\n# G-007: synthesize-spec (STRICT)\nSYNTHESIZE_SPEC_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"step\", \"source_skill\", \"cli_name\", \"synthesis_version\", \"placeholder_count\"],\n    min_lines=20,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"zero_placeholders\",\n            check_fn=lambda c: \"{{SC_PLACEHOLDER:\" not in c,\n            failure_message=\"Placeholder sentinels {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} must be removed before passing gate\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n\n# G-008: (EXIT_RECOMMENDATION + step-count consistency, STRICT)\n# G-009: (approval_status, STANDARD)\n# G-010: (EXIT_RECOMMENDATION + zero placeholders + brainstorm section, STRICT)\n# G-011: panel-review (STRICT)\nBRAINSTORM_GAPS_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"step\", \"source_skill\", \"cli_name\"],\n    min_lines=10,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STANDARD\",\n    semantic_checks=None,\n)\n\nPANEL_REVIEW_GATE = GateCriteria(\n    required_frontmatter_fields=[\"step\", \"source_skill\", \"cli_name\", \"iteration\", \"convergence_state\"],\n    min_lines=20,\n    enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\",\n    semantic_checks=[\n        SemanticCheck(\n            name=\"convergence_terminal\",\n            check_fn=lambda c: _check_convergence_terminal(c),\n            failure_message=\"convergence_state must be 'converged' or 'blocked' (terminal states only)\",\n        ),\n    ],\n)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Helper functions for semantic checks\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _check_min_sections(content: str, min_count: int) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Return True if content has at least min_count ## section headers.\"\"\"\n    sections = re.findall(r\"^#{2,3}\\s+\\S\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n    return len(sections) >= min_count\n\n\ndef _check_convergence_terminal(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Return True if convergence_state is a terminal state (converged or blocked).\"\"\"\n    match = re.search(r\"convergence_state\\s*:\\s*(\\w+)\", content)\n    if not match:\n        return False\n    state = match.group(1).lower()\n    return state in (\"converged\", \"blocked\")\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Gate registry\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nGATE_REGISTRY: dict[str, GateCriteria] = {\n    \"validate-config\": VALIDATE_CONFIG_GATE,\n    \"discover-components\": DISCOVER_COMPONENTS_GATE,\n    \"analyze-workflow\": ANALYZE_WORKFLOW_GATE,\n    \"design-pipeline\": DESIGN_PIPELINE_GATE,\n    \"synthesize-spec\": SYNTHESIZE_SPEC_GATE,\n    \"brainstorm-gaps\": BRAINSTORM_GAPS_GATE,\n    \"panel-review\": PANEL_REVIEW_GATE,\n}\n\n\ndef get_gate_criteria(step_name: str) -> GateCriteria:\n    \"\"\"Look up gate criteria by step name.\n\n    Raises:\n        KeyError: If step_name is not registered.\n    \"\"\"\n    if step_name not in GATE_REGISTRY:\n        raise KeyError(f\"No gate criteria registered for step: '{step_name}'\")\n    return GATE_REGISTRY[step_name]\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Gate functions (return tuple[bool, str] per NFR-004)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef gate_analyze_workflow(artifact_path: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Gate for analyze-workflow step (STRICT).\n\n    Checks:\n    - File exists\n    - Minimum 5 section headers\n    \"\"\"\n    if not artifact_path.exists():\n        return False, f\"Artifact not found: {artifact_path}\"\n    content = artifact_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    section_count = len(re.findall(r\"^#{2,3}\\s+\\S\", content, re.MULTILINE))\n    if section_count < 5:\n        return False, f\"Insufficient section_count: found {section_count}, need ≥5\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef gate_design_pipeline(artifact_path: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Gate for design-pipeline step (STRICT).\n\n    Checks:\n    - File exists\n    - Required frontmatter fields present\n    \"\"\"\n    if not artifact_path.exists():\n        return False, f\"Artifact not found: {artifact_path}\"\n    content = artifact_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    required = {\"step\", \"source_skill\", \"cli_name\", \"pipeline_steps\", \"gate_count\"}\n    found: set[str] = set()\n    in_fm = False\n (T04.03)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# Remediation hints by check name\n_REMEDIATION_HINTS: dict[str, str] = {\n    \"has_valid_yaml_config\": \"Add required fields (workflow_path, cli_name, output_dir) to the YAML config\",\n    \"has_component_inventory\": \"Ensure the workflow directory contains at least one component with SKILL.md\",\n \"has_approval_status\": \"Add 'approval_status: pending|approved|rejected' to the artifact frontmatter\",\n    \"has_exit_recommendation\": \"Add 'EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE' or 'EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT' to the artifact\",\n    \"has_zero_placeholders\": \"Replace all {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels with actual content\",\n    \"has_brainstorm_section\": \"Add Section 12 (Brainstorm / Gaps Analysis) to the artifact\",\n    \"has_quality_scores\": \"Add quality score fields (clarity, completeness, testability, consistency, overall) to the artifact\",\n    \"has_criticals_addressed\": \"Resolve all CRITICAL items by marking them [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\",\n    \"has_return_type_pattern\": \"Ensure return type pattern tuple[bool, str] is present in the artifact\",\n    \"has_step_count_consistency\": \"Reconcile declared step count with actual step definitions in the artifact\",\n    \"section_count\": \"Add more section headers (##) to reach the minimum required count\",\n    \"zero_placeholders\": \"Replace all {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels with actual content\",\n    \"convergence_terminal\": \"Set convergence_state to 'converged' or 'blocked' before passing this gate\",\n}\n\n_DEFAULT_REMEDIATION = \"Review the artifact against the gate specification and correct the identified issue\"\n\n\ndef format_gate_failure(\n    gate_id: str,\n    check_name: str,\n    diagnostic: str,\n    artifact_path: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format a gate failure into a human-readable multi-line string.\n\n    Args:\n        gate_id: Gate identifier (e.g. 'G-003').\n        check_name: Name of the failing semantic check.\n        diagnostic: Diagnostic message from the check.\n        artifact_path: Path to the artifact that failed the {remediation}\"\n    )\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Semantic check helper functions (FR-047, AC-004)\n# All return tuple[bool, str] — (passed, diagnostic_message)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef has_valid_yaml_config(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that YAML frontmatter contains workflow_path, cli_name, output_dir.\"\"\"\n    required = {\"workflow_path\", \"cli_name\", \"output_dir\"}\n    found: set[str] = set()\n    in_frontmatter = False\n    lines = content.splitlines()\n    fence_count = 0\n    for line in lines:\n        stripped = line.strip()\n        if stripped == \"---\":\n            fence_count += 1\n            if fence_count == 1:\n                in_frontmatter = True\n            elif fence_count == 2:\n                break\n            continue\n        if in_frontmatter and \":\" in stripped:\n            key = stripped.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n            if key:\n                found.add(key)\n    missing = required - found\n    if missing:\n        return False, f\"Missing required config fields: {sorted(missing)}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_component_inventory(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that inventory lists ≥1 component with SKILL.md reference.\"\"\"\n    # re.IGNORECASE)\n        or re.search(r\"SKILL\\.md\", content)\n    )\n    if not has_components:\n        return False, \"Inventory does not list any components with SKILL.md\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_required_analysis_sections(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for all 7 required section headers in analysis output.\"\"\"\n    required_sections = [\n        \"Source Components\",\n        \"Step Graph\",\n        \"Parallel Groups\",\n        \"Gates Summary\",\n        \"Data Flow\",\n        \"Classifications\",\n        \"Recommendations\",\n    ]\n    missing = []\n    for section in required_sections:\n        if not re.search(re.escape(section), content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            missing.append(section)\n    if missing:\n        return False, (approved/rejected/pending).\"\"\"\n    if re.search(r\"approval_status\\s*:\", content):\n        return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing required field: approval_status\"\n\n\ndef has_exit_recommendation(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that EXIT_RECOMMENDATION marker is present.\"\"\"\n    if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION\" in content:\n        return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing EXIT_RECOMMENDATION marker\"\n\n\ndef has_zero_placeholders(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that no {{SC_PLACEHOLDER:*}} sentinels remain.\"\"\"\n    match = re.search(r\"\\{\\{SC_PLACEHOLDER:[^}]*\\}\\}\", content)\n    if match:\n        return False, f\"Placeholder sentinel found: {match.group(0)}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_brainstorm_section(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that Section 12 (brainstorm / gaps) is present.\"\"\"\n    # Accept various heading patterns for brainstorm/gaps section\n    patterns = [\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)#{1,3}\\s+(?:Section\\s+12|Brainstorm|Gaps|Gap Analysis|Missing)\",\n    ]\n    for pattern in patterns:\n        if re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing brainstorm/gaps section (Section 12)\"\n\n\ndef has_quality_scores(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for quality score fields: clarity, completeness, testability, consistency, overall.\"\"\"\n    required_scores = [\"clarity\", \"completeness\", \"testability\", \"consistency\", \"overall\"]\n    missing = []\n    for score in required_scores:\n        if not re.search(rf\"\\b{score}\\b\\s*:\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n            missing.append(score)\n    if missing:\n        return False, f\"Missing quality score fields: {missing}\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_criticals_addressed(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check that all CRITICAL items are marked [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED].\"\"\"\n    # Find all CRITICAL markers\n    critical_markers = re.findall(r\"CRITICAL\", content)\n    if not critical_markers:\n        return True, \"\"  # No criticals to address\n    # Check that each CRITICAL has an adjacent [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\n    unresolved = re.findall(\n        r\"CRITICAL(?!.*?\\[(?:INCORPORATED|DISMISSED)\\])\",\n        content,\n    )\n    if unresolved:\n        return False, f\"Found {len(unresolved)} CRITICAL item(s) not marked [INCORPORATED] or [DISMISSED]\"\n    return True, \"\"\n\n\ndef has_return_type_pattern(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check for return type pattern (tuple[bool, str] or return type annotation).\"\"\"\n    if re.search(r\"tuple\\[bool,\\s*str\\]|-> tuple|return.*bool.*str\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n        return True, \"\"\n    return False, \"Missing return type pattern (tuple[bool, str])\"\n\n\ndef has_step_count_consistency(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n    \"\"\"Check timeout_s: float, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> _T:\n    \"\"\"Run *fn* in a thread and raise TimeoutError if it exceeds *timeout_s* seconds.\n\n    Used to enforce:\n      - SC-001: validate_and_configure() ≤ 30 s\n      - SC-002: discover_components()     ≤ 60 s\n\n    Args:\n        fn: Callable to execute.\n        timeout_s: Maximum allowed wall-clock seconds.\n        *args / **kwargs: Forwarded to *fn*.\n\n    Returns:\n        The return value of *fn*.\n\n    Raises:\n        TimeoutError: If *fn* does not complete within *timeout_s* seconds.\n    \"\"\"\n    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as pool:\n        future = pool.submit(fn, *args, **kwargs)\n        try:\n            return future.result(timeout=timeout_s)\n        except concurrent.futures.TimeoutError:\n            raise TimeoutError(\n                f\"Operation timed out after {timeout_s}s\"\n            )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# _determine_status()\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _determine_status(\n    exit_code: int,\n    timed_out: bool,\n    stdout: str = \"\",\n    artifact_path: Optional[Path] = None,\n) -> PortifyStatus:\n    \"\"\"Classify a step outcome from exit code, EXIT_RECOMMENDATION, and artifact.\n\n    Classification rules (priority order):\n      1. exit 124 (or timed_out=True)  → TIMEOUT\n      2. exit non-zero                 → ERROR\n      3. exit 0 + marker + artifact    → PASS\n      4. exit 0 + no marker + artifact → PASS_NO_SIGNAL  (triggers retry)\n      5. exit 0 + artifact, no result  → PASS_NO_REPORT  (no retry)\n      6. exit 0 + no artifact          → PASS_NO_REPORT\n    \"\"\"\n    if timed_out or exit_code == EXIT_CODE_TIMEOUT:\n        return PortifyStatus.TIMEOUT\n\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return PortifyStatus.ERROR\n\n    # exit 0 paths\n    has_marker = EXIT_RECOMMENDATION_MARKER in stdout\n    artifact_exists = artifact_path is not None and Path(artifact_path).exists()\n\n    if has_marker and artifact_exists:\n        return PortifyStatus.PASS\n\n    if not has_marker and artifact_exists:\n        return PortifyStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL  # triggers retry\n\n    # No artifact (or no result file) → no retry\n    return PortifyStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Return contract helpers (T03.10)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _build_resume_command(step_id: str, cli_name: str = \"portify\") -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build the exact CLI command string for resuming from a step.\"\"\"\n    return f\"superclaude cli-portify run --resume {step_id}\"\n\n\ndef _calculate_suggested_resume_budget(steps: list[PortifyStep]) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Calculate suggested_resume_budget = remaining_steps_count * 25.\n\n    remaining = steps with PENDING or INCOMPLETE status.\n    \"\"\"\n    remaining = sum(\n        1 for s in steps\n        if s.status in (PortifyStatus.PENDING, PortifyStatus.INCOMPLETE)\n    )\n    return remaining * 25\n\n\ndef _emit_return_contract(\n    workdir: Path,\n    outcome: PortifyOutcome,\n    steps: list[PortifyStep],\n    completed_steps: list[str],\n    resume_from_step_id: str = \"\",\n) -> Path:\n    \"\"\"Emit return-contract.yaml to the workdir on ALL outcome paths (SC-011).\n\n    Returns the path to the emitted file.\n    \"\"\"\n    remaining_steps = [s.step_id for s in steps if s.status in (PortifyStatus.PENDING, PortifyStatus.INCOMPLETE)]\n    resume_command = _build_resume_command(resume_from_step_id) if resume_from_step_id else \"\"\n    suggested_budget = _calculate_suggested_resume_budget(steps)\n\n    contract = {\n        \"outcome\": outcome.value,\n        \"completed_steps\": completed_steps,\n        \"remaining_steps\": remaining_steps,\n        \"resume_command\": resume_command,\n        \"suggested_resume_budget\": suggested_budget,\n    }\n\n    workdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    contract_path = workdir / \"return-contract.yaml\"\n    with open(contract_path, \"w\") as fh:\n        yaml.safe_dump(contract, fh, default_flow_style=False)\n    return contract_path\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PortifyExecutor\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass PortifyExecutor:\n    \"\"\"Sequential pipeline executor for CLI Portify.\n\n    Usage:\n        executor = PortifyExecutor(steps, workdir, dry_run=False)\n        outcome = executor.run()\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        steps: list[PortifyStep],\n        workdir: Path,\n        *,\n        dry_run: bool = False,\n        resume_from: str = \"\",\n        turn_budget: int = 200,\n        step_runner: Optional[Callable[[PortifyStep], tuple[int, str, bool]]] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Args:\n            steps: Ordered list of steps in registered order.\n            workdir: Working directory for artifacts and return contract.\n            dry_run: If True, filter execution to DRY_RUN_PHASE_TYPES only.\n            resume_from: Step ID to resume from (skip all prior steps).\n            turn_budget: Total Claude-invocation budget (TurnLedger).\n            step_runner: Optional callable (step) -> (exit_code, stdout, timed_out).\n                         Used for testing; real runs use subprocess.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.steps = steps\n        self.workdir = workdir\n        self.dry_run = dry_run\n        self.resume_from = resume_from\n        self._ledger = TurnLedger(total_budget=turn_budget)\n        self._step_runner = step_runner\n        self._interrupted: bool = False\n        self._completed_steps: list[str] = []\n        self._step_results: dict[str, PortifyStepResult] = {}\n\n        # Signal handler integration points (T03.04 / T03.09)\n        self._prev_sigint: Optional[Callable] = None\n        self._prev_sigterm: Optional[Callable] = None\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Signal handling (T03.09)\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _install_signal_handlers(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Register SIGINT / SIGTERM handlers for graceful shutdown.\"\"\"\n        def _handle(signum: int, frame) -> None:\n            self._interrupted = True\n\n        try:\n            self._prev_sigint = signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handle)\n            self._prev_sigterm = signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle)\n        except (OSError, ValueError):\n            # Non-main thread or OS doesn't support — skip gracefully\n            pass\n\n    def _restore_signal_handlers(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Restore prior signal handlers after execution.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            if self._prev_sigint is not None:\n                signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._prev_sigint)\n            if self._prev_sigterm is not None:\n                signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._prev_sigterm)\n        except (OSError, ValueError):\n            pass\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Step filtering\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _should_execute(self, step: PortifyStep) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if this step should be executed given current mode.\"\"\"\n        if self.dry_run:\n            return step.phase_type in DRY_RUN_PHASE_TYPES\n        return True\n\n    def _should_skip_for_resume(self, step: PortifyStep, resume_started: bool) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if this step should be skipped because --resume hasn't reached it.\"\"\"\n        return not resume_started\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Step execution\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _execute_step(self, step: PortifyStep) -> PortifyStatus:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single step with retry logic (retry_limit=1 for PASS_NO_SIGNAL).\"\"\"\n        if self._step_runner is not None:\n            exit_code, stdout, timed_out = self._step_runner(step)\n        else:\n            # Default: no-op (real subprocess invocation belongs in process.py)\n            exit_code, stdout, timed_out = 0, \"\", False\n\n        artifact_path = step.output_file\n        status = _determine_status(exit_code, timed_out, stdout, artifact_path)\n        self._ledger.consume(1)\n\n        # Retry once on PASS_NO_SIGNAL (retry_limit=1 per NFR-002)\n        if status == PortifyStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL and step.retry_limit >= 1:\n            if self._ledger.can_launch():\n                if self._step_runner is not None:\n                    exit_code, stdout, timed_out = self._step_runner(step)\n                else:\n                    exit_code, stdout, timed_out = 0, \"\", False\n                self._ledger.consume(1)\n                status = _determine_status(exit_code, timed_out, stdout, artifact_path)\n\n        return status\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Main execution loop\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def run(self) -> PortifyOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Execute all steps sequentially. Returns pipeline outcome.\"\"\"\n        self._install_signal_handlers()\n        outcome = PortifyOutcome.SUCCESS\n        resume_started = (self.resume_from == \"\")  # True if no resume target\n\n        try:\n            for step in self.steps:\n                # Check for interrupt before each step\n                if self._interrupted:\n                    outcome = PortifyOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                    break\n\n                # Resume skip logic\n                if not resume_started:\n                    if step.step_id == self.resume_from:\n                        resume_started = True\n                    else:\n                        step.status = PortifyStatus.SKIPPED\n                        continue\n\n                # Dry-run filtering\n                if not self._should_execute(step):\n                    step.status = PortifyStatus.SKIPPED\n                    continue\n\n                # Budget check before launch (FR-040)\n                if not self._ledger.can_launch():\n                    outcome = PortifyOutcome.HALTED\n                    break\n\n                step.status = PortifyStatus.RUNNING\n                status = self._execute_step(step)\n                step.status = status\n\n                if status in (PortifyStatus.PASS, PortifyStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL, PortifyStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT):\n                    self._completed_steps.append(step.step_id)\n                elif status == PortifyStatus.TIMEOUT:\n                    outcome = PortifyOutcome.TIMEOUT\n                    break\n                elif status == PortifyStatus.ERROR:\n                    outcome = PortifyOutcome.FAILURE\n                    break\n\n                # Check interrupt after step completes\n                if self._interrupted:\n                    outcome = PortifyOutcome.INTERRUPTED\n                    break\n\n        finally:\n            self._restore_signal_handlers()\n            # Determine resume step (first non-completed step)\n            resume_step = \"\"\n            for s in self.steps:\n                if s.status in (PortifyStatus.PENDING, PortifyStatus.INCOMPLETE):\n                    resume_step = s.step_id\n                    break\n            _emit_return_contract(\n                workdir=self.workdir,\n                outcome=outcome if not self.dry_run else PortifyOutcome.DRY_RUN,\n                steps=self.steps,\n                completed_steps=self._completed_steps,\n                resume_from_step_id=resume_step,\n            )\n\n        return outcome\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Phase 5 Step Execution Functions (T05.01, T05.02, T05.04)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef execute_protocol_mapping_step(\n    config_cli_name: str,\n    inventory: list,\n    workdir: Path,\n    process_runner: Optional[Callable[[str, Path], tuple[int, str, bool]]] = None,\n) -> PortifyStepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute the protocol-mapping step (T05.01, FR-013, FR-014).\n\n    Runs Claude via subprocess to produce ``workdir/protocol-map.md`` with\n    required YAML frontmatter and EXIT_RECOMMENDATION marker. PortifyPhaseType.PREREQUISITES,\n    PortifyPhaseType.ANALYSIS,\n    PortifyPhaseType.USER_REVIEW,\n    PortifyPhaseType.SPECIFICATION,\n})\n\n# Exit code constants\nEXIT_CODE_TIMEOUT: int = 124\nEXIT_RECOMMENDATION_MARKER: str = \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION:\"\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Phase 5: STEP_REGISTRY — step definitions with PortifyPhaseType.PREREQUISITES,\n        \"timeout_s\": 30,\n        \"retry_limit\": 0,\n    },\n    \"discover-components\": {\n        \"step_id\": \"discover-components\",\n        \"phase_type\": PortifyPhaseType.PREREQUISITES,\n        \"timeout_s\": 60,\n        \"retry_limit\": 0,\n    },\n    \"protocol-mapping\": {\n        \"step_id\": \"protocol-mapping\",\n        \"phase_type\":
